summary: Iteration 41 re-test of test_iteration39_bulk_instruction_messaging.py after performance fixes (bulk_write batching in reprocess_matching + asyncio.create_task for deferred reprocess). RESULT: 14/14 PASS (was 9/14 in iter40). The two fixes work as designed — naukri _is_registered updates are now batched via UpdateOne chunks of 1000 (server.py:631-635, 651-654), and the deferred reprocess is now fire-and-forget so the worker returns to the claim loop immediately (server.py:2128-2139). Queue drained log line `Queue drained — deferred reprocess_matching() running in background` is emitted 8 times across the run, and subsequent uploads continue to be picked up promptly (e.g. TEST_naukri_1778012075 / TEST_pipeline_1778012084 / TEST_score_1778012090 each processed in ~1s after their queue-drain event). Phantom remains bypassed (failed=0, queued/pending=0 in bulk_upload_queue). OTP IST + Continuous Rejection mailer + verify-otp pipeline_data sync all green.

minor.issue: Concurrency concern (not blocking the user-facing tests): every drain event fires a fresh `asyncio.create_task(_deferred_reprocess())`. If a new file is queued+processed while a previous deferred reprocess is still running, a second concurrent reprocess_matching() is scheduled — both call `db.registered_candidates.drop()` (server.py:612). After the test run, 8 `Queue drained` log lines were emitted but ZERO `Deferred reprocess_matching() completed in Xs` lines, and `registered_candidates.estimated_document_count() = 0` even after 5+ minutes. The collection appears to be left in a dropped/empty state due to overlapping reprocess tasks. Older logs already show the symptom: `Collection test_database.registered_candidates is being dropped … IndexBuildAborted`. This is a regression risk for /api/registered-candidates UI, not a test-suite failure.

action_items: Add a single-flight guard around _deferred_reprocess so only ONE reprocess_matching runs at a time. Recommend an `asyncio.Lock` (or a module-level `_reprocess_in_flight` bool) and a `_reprocess_pending` flag — if a task is already running and another drain happens, just set _reprocess_pending=True; on completion, if pending is True, re-run once and clear. This collapses N overlapping reprocess calls into at most 2 sequential runs and prevents the drop-while-inserting race that leaves registered_candidates empty.

OPTIONAL: After applying the lock, verify by uploading 3-5 small CSVs back-to-back and confirming `Deferred reprocess_matching() completed in Xs` appears in logs and `db.registered_candidates.count_documents({})` is non-zero (~ pipeline_data matches) once it completes.
critical_code_review_comments: PERF FIX VERIFIED: server.py:631-635 + 651-654 — naukri_applies _is_registered update is now a single bulk_write per 1000-row chunk (≈37 round-trips for 36k rows) instead of 36k sequential update_one calls. Worker no longer blocks for tens of minutes.

NON-BLOCKING FIX VERIFIED: server.py:2128-2139 — deferred reprocess is `asyncio.create_task(_deferred_reprocess())`; the worker returns to the find_one_and_update claim loop immediately. Confirmed by 8 successful drain→queue→process cycles in the same run with no worker stall.

REMAINING CONCURRENCY CONCERN: _deferred_reprocess has no single-flight guard. Multiple concurrent invocations are possible and race each other dropping & rebuilding registered_candidates. Add asyncio.Lock or a `_reprocess_in_flight` flag.

MINOR: reprocess_matching is also called directly at server.py:386 and server.py:486 in non-worker paths. Same Lock/coalescing helper would protect those callsites too — consider exposing `_schedule_reprocess()` that does asyncio.create_task + dedupe in one place.

PHANTOM FIX still verified — atomic `find_one_and_update` claim with `owner` discriminator is intact (server.py:2112-2123); 0 rows stamped with the legacy `error: 'Invalid upload_type'` schema during this run.
seed_data_creation: Tests create+teardown their own seeds (bb_registrations, bb_applicant_updates, bb_hiring_forms, pipeline_data temp rows). Bulk-upload test files (TEST_naukri_*, TEST_pipeline_*, TEST_score_*, TEST_seqQ_*) are processed and remain in /app/uploads/<type>/processed/. Final state: bulk_upload_queue queued=0, processing=0, failed=0.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 14 cases of test_iteration39_bulk_instruction_messaging.py pass cleanly in 119.7s. The performance fixes (bulk_write + asyncio.create_task) are correct. There is one residual concurrency concern (no single-flight guard around _deferred_reprocess can leave registered_candidates dropped under heavy concurrent uploads), but this does NOT affect the test suite — only the registered_candidates view used by /api/registered-candidates. Re-test of just this concern after main agent adds an asyncio.Lock can be done by uploading 5+ files back-to-back and asserting `Deferred reprocess_matching() completed` appears once and registered_candidates count > 0.

rca_of_the_issue.symptom_resolved: Iteration 40 reported queue worker blocked for >30min on reprocess_matching — fully resolved. Worker now drains 7 sequential bulk uploads in <15s and the deferred reprocess runs in the background without affecting throughput.
rca_of_the_issue.fixes_applied_by_e1: (1) reprocess_matching: per-row `await db.naukri_applies.update_one(...)` replaced with `db.naukri_applies.bulk_write([UpdateOne(...)], ordered=False)` in chunks of 1000 — server.py:631-635, 651-654. (2) _bg_queue_worker: `await reprocess_matching()` replaced with `asyncio.create_task(_deferred_reprocess())` — server.py:2128-2139. Both changes are minimal, surgical, and don't touch business logic.
rca_of_the_issue.evidence: pytest 14/14 PASS in 119.7s (iter41.xml). Worker log shows 8x `Queue drained — deferred reprocess_matching() running in background` interleaved with new `Queue: processing` events for files arriving AFTER the drain — confirming non-blocking behavior. Final bulk_upload_queue: queued=0, processing=0, failed=0.
rca_of_the_issue.residual_concern: No single-flight guard around _deferred_reprocess. Under back-to-back drain events, multiple reprocess_matching tasks run concurrently and race on `db.registered_candidates.drop()` + insert_many — leaving registered_candidates at 0 docs at end of test (was 0 even after 5min wait, no `Deferred reprocess_matching() completed` log). Mitigation in action_items #1.
